Guideline: Neutral — Swedish du-reform (1960s) makes informal du universal and non-disrespectful. Use du throughout. No Sie/vous equivalent needed.
Professional, clear, and direct. Neutral on pay gap data.
Use hen or gender-neutral constructions where natural. Avoid defaulting to masculine forms.
Sentence case throughout. Swedish does not capitalize job titles, common nouns, months, or languages. Never use title case.
Straight double quotes ("example") used consistently in existing TM.
Standard Swedish punctuation. Percent: 15 % (space before sign — Swedish convention). Decimal separator: comma (,). Thousands: space.
{variable} placeholders preserved unchanged. Date: YYYY-MM-DD (ISO standard, dominant in Swedish business).
Swedish compounds written as single words: Lönegap, Medarbetargrupp, Jämförelsegrupp, Personalanalys. Do not split with spaces.

Kept in English: PayAnalytics, gap (as in lönegap — do not replace with löneskillnad), bonus, KPI, p-value, API, CSV, feedback, benchmark (acceptable in UI labels). Swedish readily adopts English HR terminology.
Swedish V2 rule: imperative for UI labels: Lägg till filter, Exportera lista, Jämför. Verb placed first.
Actions: Lägg till [noun], Ta bort [noun], Exportera [noun]. Confirmations: Är du säker på att du vill...? Success: ...[noun] har sparats.

Pay gap=Lönegap (keeps English 'gap'); Adjusted pay gap=Justerat lönegap; Unadjusted pay gap=Ojusterat lönegap; Gender pay gap=Lönegap mellan könen; Pay band=Lönespann; Salary range=Löneintervall; Compensation=Ersättning; Salary=Lön; Raise=Löneökning; Benchmark=Riktmärkning; Pay equity=Rättvis lönesättning; Equal pay=Lika lön; Job evaluation=Jobbutvärdering; Workforce analytics=Personalanalys; Headcount=Antal anställda; Dataset=Datamängd; Comparison group=Jämförelsegrupp; Employee group=Medarbetargrupp; Maternity leave=Föräldraledighet; Bonus=Bonus (kept); Compa-ratio=Compa-ratio (kept)
+8% average length vs. English. Close to English in length — good fit for UI strings.
